raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I paid {$5100.00} to a XXXX company ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX my American Express card for a complete XXXX business setup package. This full package was supposed to include : Visa application and processing XXXX ID setup and medical appointment coordination Personal and business bank account setup Agent support for all appointments and follow-ups Government liaison for the entire business onboarding process Ongoing client service for the setup period The only thing that was delivered was the business license which is one of the earliest and easiest steps of this type of service. As soon as the visa attempt failed, the company abandoned the process completely. They did not support me with XXXX ID, medical, banking appointments, or follow-ups of any kind. They essentially said : The visa was rejected thats it, and treated the rest of the package as complete, which is simply not reasonable. 

To illustrate how minimal their delivery was : this is like hiring a contractor to build your house, paying for a full project including plumbing, electrical, interior, etc., and all they do is file a permit with the city. Then they say We tried but were keeping the full money. Thats how absurd this situation is. 

I disputed the charge with Amex. Amex originally credited the funds back, but then reversed their decision after the merchant claimed the service was non-refundable. I explained clearly to Amex that I wasnt disputing based on refund terms Im disputing because the full package service was not rendered. 

Ive attached a XXXX document showing : The XXXX conversation with the merchant ( showing abandonment of service after visa rejection ) Payment proof Timeline of events Summary of the promised vs. delivered services The Amex messages during the dispute, including a statement from the rep that if the merchant refuses to refund, we cant do much. 

This is a clear example of a service not being delivered as promised, and I am now stuck with a {$5100.00} charge for something that was never fully provided.
